disclaimer: Routing aid only. It must tell users to check with a building control body or competent professional before relying on the result.

higher_risk_building.question: Is the building at least 7 storeys or at least 18m high and in a relevant residential/hospital/care use?
higher_risk_building.outcome: Route to BSR higher-risk building guidance before any normal route.

started_without_approval.question: Has work already started or finished without approval/certification?
started_without_approval.outcome: Show regularisation/completion evidence route and warn that normal notice routes may no longer apply.

listed_or_conservation.question: Is the building listed, in a conservation area, a flat, leasehold, or subject to planning conditions?
listed_or_conservation.outcome: Keep building regs answer separate but add planning/listed/freeholder warning.

likely_building_control_needed.headline: Likely building control approval needed
likely_building_control_needed.cta: Prepare drawings/evidence and contact local authority building control or a registered building control approver before starting.

competent_person_route_possible.headline: A competent person scheme may cover this
competent_person_route_possible.cta: Use a properly registered installer and keep the compliance certificate. If not using one, check the building control route before starting.

may_be_exempt_or_minor.headline: May be exempt/minor, but check the details
may_be_exempt_or_minor.cta: Keep product/installer evidence and check if the work is part of a wider project.

bsr_route.headline: Higher-risk building route: check BSR guidance
bsr_route.cta: Do not use the standard domestic route until the higher-risk building status is resolved.

regularisation_or_evidence_problem.headline: Work may need regularisation or evidence recovery
regularisation_or_evidence_problem.cta: Contact the local authority building control team and gather photos, invoices, certificates and structural details.
